summary: Recent findings emphasize critical roles of multiple transcription factors such as MSX1/MSX2, DLX proteins, among others, in regulating odontoblast development; specifically supporting C-MSX2 due to broader implications seen across both tooth structure components including dentin and enamel. This underscores complex regulatory networks involved rather than focusing solely on indirect contributors or less directly linked genes like PHEX. To ensure accuracy, consider re-evaluating based on these comprehensive genomic data points related closely tied to primary cell functions during mineralizing tissue genesis.
